{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42663412?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/shanjijian", "id": 42663412, "login": "shanjijian", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjQyNjYzNDEy", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/shanjijian", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-54540", "cvss": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-78", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')"}], "description": "### Summary

Pheditor 2.0.4 has an authenticated terminal command whitelist bypass.

The terminal feature checks whether the submitted command starts with one of the configured `TERMINAL_COMMANDS` values, then passes the full command string to `shell_exec()`. Shell command substitution such as `$()` is not blocked, so an authenticated user with the `terminal` permission can bypass a restricted command allowlist and execute arbitrary shell commands as the web server user.

### Details

Tested repository:

https://github.com/pheditor/pheditor

Tested commit:

`62b43df7cb8956a9b0deb9bec278ca8676c890c5`

Affected version:

Pheditor 2.0.4

Relevant code in `pheditor.php`:

- The terminal handler receives `$_POST['command']` and stores it in `$command`.
- It blocks only `&`, `;`, and `||`.
- It checks whether `$command` starts with one of the configured values in `TERMINAL_COMMANDS`.
- It then passes the full command string to `shell_exec()`.

Relevant logic:

```php
$command = $_POST['command'];

if (strpos($command, '&') !== false || strpos($command, ';') !== false || strpos($command, '||') !== false) {
    echo json_error("Illegal character(s) in command (& ; ||)\n");
    exit;
}

foreach ($terminal_commands as $value) {
    $value = trim($value);

    if (strlen($command) >= strlen($value) && substr($command, 0, strlen($value)) == $value) {
        $command_found = true;
        break;
    }
}

$output = shell_exec((empty($dir) ? null : 'cd ' . escapeshellarg($dir) . ' && ') . $command . ' && echo \ ; pwd');
```

Because the whitelist check is prefix-based and the full command is executed by a shell, a command such as `ls$(...)` passes when `ls` is allowed, while the command substitution is still executed by the shell.

### PoC

This was reproduced locally with Docker and PHP 8.3.

For a strict test, the configured command allowlist was changed to only allow `ls`:

```php
define('TERMINAL_COMMANDS', 'ls');
```

Control request:

```text
command=whoami
```

Observed result:

```text
Command not allowed
Available commands:
ls
```

Bypass request:

```text
command=ls$(printf pheditor-terminal-bypass >/lab/app/site/proof.txt)
```

Observed result:

```text
proof.txt is created with the content:
pheditor-terminal-bypass
```

This shows that even when only `ls` is allowed, arbitrary shell commands can still be executed through command substitution.

### Impact

An authenticated user with the `terminal` permission can bypass the intended `TERMINAL_COMMANDS` restriction and execute arbitrary shell commands as the web server user.

This affects deployments where administrators rely on `TERMINAL_COMMANDS` to restrict terminal access to a small set of safe commands.

Suggested fixes:

- Avoid passing user-controlled command strings to `shell_exec()`.
- Parse the command into executable and arguments.
- Require an exact command name match instead of prefix matching.
- Execute without a shell, for example with an argument-array based process API.
- If shell execution remains necessary, reject shell metacharacters comprehensively, including command substitution syntax.
- Consider disabling the terminal feature by default.

Reporter credit requested:

shanjijian <shanjijian@gmail.com>", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-16T20:01:05Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-54540"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-16T20:01:05Z", "references": ["https://github.com/pheditor/pheditor/security/advisories/GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57", "https://github.com/pheditor/pheditor/releases/tag/2.0.5", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/pheditor/pheditor/security-advisories/GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/pheditor/pheditor", "summary": "Pheditor has an authenticated terminal command whitelist bypass", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-16T20:01:05Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-9643-6xjp-vx57", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.0.5", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "pheditor/pheditor"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2.0.4"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Pheditor has an authenticated terminal command whitelist bypass
